title: monsonia lheritieri
intro: Monsonia lheritieri, a species of flowering plant in the family Moringaceae, is commonly known as the 'wild moringa' due to its similarities with the moringa plant. Traditionally, the leaves and roots of Monsonia lheritieri have been used in herbal medicine for their potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Monsonia lheritieri are typically dried, ground into a powder, or made into a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
